product: NetApp ONTAP REST API — volume provisioning with asynchronous jobs

auth.note: sent preemptively on every request; credentials never appear in exception text

lookup_volume.client_rule: num_records 0 means the volume must be created; 1 means idempotent hit (reuse uuid, no POST); more than 1 is a resolution error

resolve_svm.client_rule: exactly one record required; anything else is a resolution error and no volume is created

resolve_aggregate.client_rule: one GET per requested aggregate name, in spec order; exactly one record required per name

body.name: <volume name>
body.nas: {path: <junction>} — key OMITTED entirely when no junction path is requested

request.path: exactly the href from job._links.self.href, byte-for-byte (the fixture href carries its own query string; never rebuild the URL from the uuid or append parameters)

poll_job.on_failure: job document carries error {code, message}; raise JobFailedError with that code and message and issue no further volume requests
poll_job.pacing: sleep(1.0) via the injected sleep callable before every poll except the first; max_polls bounds total polls, and exhausting it raises JobFailedError whose message contains 'poll budget' and the bound

errors.client_rule: non-2xx responses raise OntapApiError with status, code, message, target; str(error) never contains the password

error.message: Cannot provision volume 'vol_bad': insufficient space in aggregate 'aggr_ssd_01'
